content.verdict: Finch Bay is the only true beachfront hotel on Santa Cruz and the most relaxed honeymoon base in Galápagos. Reached by water-taxi across Academy Bay from Puerto Ayora, the property is hidden in a mangrove forest with a small private beach where marine iguanas warm themselves on the rocks at low tide. The 27 rooms are simple but elegant in a Pacific-coast vernacular — the real magic is the rhythm of the place: morning coffee with Darwin's finches, an afternoon snorkel from the beach, an evening cocktail at the open-air bar, and the family-owned Sea Lion Yacht standing by for full-day cruises to Bartolomé, Plazas, or Santa Fe. It is more affordable than Pikaia, more local in feeling, and ideal for couples who want a soft adventure honeymoon over a polished design hotel.
content.best_room: Choose an Oceanview Suite — the only category with a balcony directly facing Academy Bay and a king bed positioned to catch the sea breeze. The newer Suites also include the deepest tubs and a separate sitting area. Avoid the garden-view rooms on the back side if ocean access matters; they are lovely but feel removed from the water that defines this property.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Quito to Galápagos Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Long travel day — overnight in Quito and connect to Baltra (GPS) on a morning LATAM or Avianca flight. National park entry $200 per person on arrival. Finch Bay transfers via Itabaca Channel, a quick van ride to Puerto Ayora, and a water-taxi across the bay.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tortuga Bay & Settle In
itinerary_7_nights.description: Acclimatise with a guided walk to Tortuga Bay — the long white-sand beach where marine iguanas and reef sharks patrol the shore. Lunch at the hotel's organic-farm restaurant and an afternoon kayak around the mangroves before the sunset cocktail hour.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sea Lion Yacht — Bartolomé
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day cruise on the Sea Lion to Bartolomé Island — the iconic Pinnacle Rock viewpoint, a hike to the panoramic summit, and a snorkel with Galápagos penguins. Onboard lunch and Champagne return.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Charles Darwin Station & Highlands
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the Charles Darwin Research Station to meet the giant-tortoise breeding program, then up into the Santa Cruz highlands for lava tunnels and a working farm where wild tortoises graze freely. Late lunch and an afternoon at the pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sea Lion Yacht — North Seymour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Second day on the family yacht — North Seymour Island for blue-footed boobies in courtship dance, magnificent frigatebirds inflating their red throat sacs, and a beach landing among sea-lion colonies. Snorkel cove on the way back to Santa Cruz.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach Day & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow day at the hotel — beach loungers, a couples' massage, and an afternoon snorkel from the property's own dock. Sunset paddleboarding through the mangroves and a chef's-table dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell & Return
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final breakfast on the deck, water-taxi back to Puerto Ayora, and Finch Bay transfer to Baltra for the flight back to mainland Ecuador.

caveats: Galápagos National Park entry fee is $200 per person plus a $20 INGALA transit card, paid in cash at Baltra airport.
Finch Bay is reached by a 5-minute water-taxi from Puerto Ayora — fine for most travellers but worth knowing if you have a lot of luggage or limited mobility.
December–May is warm and calm (best for swimming and snorkeling); June–November is cool and rougher but with denser wildlife. Sea Lion Yacht itineraries shift slightly by season.
All park excursions require a certified naturalist guide — Finch Bay includes them on Sea Lion days but day-trip add-ons should be confirmed in advance.

faqs.question: Is Finch Bay good for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Yes — it is the most romantic Santa Cruz beachfront option, with mangrove privacy, a small couples-leaning crowd, and the family-owned Sea Lion Yacht giving you the cruise highlights without giving up a real bed and a private beach.

faqs.question: Land-based or cruise — which is right for us?
faqs.answer: If you want maximum islands and don't mind a small cabin, choose a live-aboard like Silver Origin or Ecoventura. If you want comfort, beach time, and a slower pace with fewer but more relaxed wildlife days, Finch Bay is the better match.

faqs.question: Will we see the iconic wildlife?
faqs.answer: Marine iguanas live on the property's beach. Sea lions are guaranteed on Sea Lion Yacht days. Blue-footed boobies and frigatebirds appear on North Seymour. Giant tortoises are a sure thing in the highlands. Penguins on Bartolomé are seasonal but common.

faqs.question: What's the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December–May for warm water and calm seas, June–November for cooler air and the most active seabird and sea-lion colonies. Honeymooners often pick March or November to balance both.

faqs.question: Are meals and excursions included?
faqs.answer: Finch Bay sells flexible packages — usually full-board with at least one Sea Lion Yacht day. Add-on cruises and private guides are available. Confirm exactly what is in your rate before booking.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly Quito or Guayaquil to Baltra (GPS), 2 hours. Finch Bay arranges the Itabaca Channel ferry, the Santa Cruz drive, and the Puerto Ayora water-taxi as a seamless transfer (about 1 hour 45 minutes total).
